label: independent
hint_value: Cut the brim off a white baseball hat, drape a sheet over the person wearing the hat, mark and cut out the eyes and nose, draw a face on the sheet, and pin the sheet to the hat with safety pins.

Looking at the steps in both options, they are almost identical except for one part. Option A says to pin the sheet to the hat, while option B says to pin it to the head.

To create the ghostly face, you cut out the eyes and nose from the sheet and draw a face. Now, where to pin the sheet? If you pin the sheet to the hat, that would mean attaching the sheet (which is now the ghost's face) to the hat. But the hat is on the head. So if you drape the sheet over the person wearing the hat, and then cut the eyes and nose, the sheet would cover their head and body. However, pinning the sheet to the hat might make sense if the hat is part of the headgear that holds the sheet in place. Alternatively, pinning the sheet to the head would secure it directly onto the person's head.

The sheet is draped over the person, then you need to secure it so that the face part stays in place. If you have a hat without a brim, maybe the sheet is pinned to the hat to make the face part stay on the head. Otherwise, if you just pin to the head, the sheet might not stay properly. So A makes sense. But the answer might be B if the sheet is pinned to the head directly. Wait, the steps in A say "pin the sheet to the hat", which would require that the hat is already on the person. The sheet is first draped over the person wearing the hat. Then you cut the eyes and nose, draw the face, and pin the sheet to the hat. That seems a bit off because once you've draped the sheet and cut the face, pinning it to the hat would secure the face part to the hat. question_text: To make a ghost costume for Halloween,

choices.A: Cut the brim off a white baseball hat, drape a sheet over the person wearing the hat, mark and cut out the eyes and nose, draw a face on the sheet, and pin the sheet to the hat with safety pins.
choices.B: Cut the brim off a white baseball hat, drape a sheet over the person wearing the hat, mark and cut out the eyes and nose, draw a face on the sheet, and pin the sheet to the head with safety pins.
